epic.title: CLI first-run onboarding & persisted default priorities
epic.summary: Add a one-time interactive onboarding to the tryaii CLI (both npm and pip) that, on first run or via `tryaii init`, asks consent to download the local embedding model, captures default quality/cost/speed priorities, and offers a quick inline route demo — then never re-prompts. Saved priorities become the routing baseline for route/eval.
epic.outcome: A developer installing tryaii for the first time is guided from install to a first successful route in one session: the model download is consented (not silent), their priority preferences are saved and applied automatically, and bare `tryaii` stops prompting once configured. Behavior stays byte-identical across the Node and Python CLIs.

stories.title: User-config persistence layer (config.json) in both SDKs
stories.summary: Add a small JSON user-config at <data-dir>/config.json with load/save/path helpers in both SDKs, storing `onboarded` and `defaultPriorities`.
stories.user_story: As a returning CLI user, I want my onboarding state and default priorities saved to disk, so that the tool remembers my setup across runs and never re-prompts unnecessarily.
requirements: Add load/save/path helpers in packages/node/src/config.ts and packages/python/tryaii/config.py.
File lives at <data-dir>/config.json, where data-dir honors TRYAII_DRE_DATA_DIR (default ~/.tryaii) in BOTH SDKs (Node currently hard-codes the default).
Schema: { "onboarded": boolean, "defaultPriorities": { "quality": int, "cost": int, "speed": int } }.
A missing or corrupt file is treated as empty / not-onboarded — load never throws.
Writes are atomic (temp + rename) and create the data dir if absent.
stories.confidence_notes: Mechanical, well-scoped; mirrors existing config patterns in both SDKs.

stories.title: Interactive onboarding flow + `tryaii init` command
stories.summary: Add `tryaii init` (always runs) and bare `tryaii` on first run (TTY only) launching a 3-step onboarding: model-download consent, default-priorities capture, and an optional quick inline route demo.
stories.user_story: As a first-time user, I want a guided setup the first time I run tryaii, so that I download the model intentionally, set my priorities, and see the router work — without reading the docs first.
requirements: Three steps: (a) consent to download the embedding model — reuses the existing `setup` warmup (Router(); router.route('warmup')); (b) capture default quality/cost/speed; (c) optional demo routing ~4 bundled sample prompts at top_k=3, printing each bestModel.
Trigger: bare `tryaii` runs onboarding ONLY when no config exists AND stdin is a TTY; otherwise it prints the global help. `tryaii init` always runs it and is re-runnable.
Non-TTY `tryaii init` prints a short notice and exits 0 — it never blocks on input.
Persist `onboarded:true` + chosen priorities right after step (b), so onboarding counts even if the demo is skipped.
Add `init` to the command set in both CLIs: a line in global HELP, a HELP_INIT page, and a COMMAND_HELP entry.
stories.confidence_notes: Flow is specified end-to-end in docs/cli-init-onboarding-plan.md; main risk is parity of prompt text.

stories.title: Saved default priorities become the route/eval baseline
stories.summary: Make route and eval fall back to the saved quality/cost/speed defaults when the flags are omitted, while keeping per-call overrides.
stories.user_story: As a configured user, I want route and eval to use my saved priorities by default, so that I don't have to pass --quality/--cost/--speed on every call.
requirements: Remove the parse-time default of 3 (Node) / set argparse default=None (Python) for --quality/--cost/--speed on route and eval, so an omitted flag is detectable.
Resolution order per dimension: explicit flag > saved default > factory 3.
An explicit flag always wins over the saved default.
Help text keeps stating 'default 3' (the factory default); saved values surface in eval's printed priorities line.
Reuse the existing Priorities 1-5 clamp for all resolved values.
stories.confidence_notes: Depends on s1 for the saved values; the omitted-vs-explicit flag detection is the crux.

stories.title: Docs + cross-SDK parity tests for the onboarding surface
stories.summary: Document `init`, update the CLI docs/help topics, and extend the parity + CLI test suites to cover the new surface.
stories.user_story: As a maintainer, I want the new init/onboarding surface documented and guarded by parity tests, so that the npm and pip CLIs cannot silently drift on it.
requirements: Add docs/cli/init.md; add an `init` row to docs/cli/README.md and to the help.md topics list.
Add "init": "HELP_INIT" to COMMAND_HELP_CONSTANTS in tests/test_parity.py so the byte-identical help guard covers it.
New tests: priority-resolution (saved becomes baseline; explicit flag wins), user-config round-trip, and non-TTY `init` prints a notice and exits 0.
The existing bare-`tryaii`-prints-HELP test stays green via the TTY gate.
Note in the env-vars doc table that Node now also reads TRYAII_DRE_DATA_DIR (for config.json).
stories.confidence_notes: Test wiring is clear; the medium reflects that exact HELP_INIT text must be authored byte-identically across both CLIs.
